stem: A pharmaceutical QC study measures drug dosage concentration for 20 samples to determine if the process variability is within ±2% of the labeled value. The sample standard deviation is 6.91 mg/mL (sample variance = 47.78). Estimate the true population standard deviation of drug dosage concentration and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Sample SD = 6.91 mg/mL. 95% CI for σ (chi-squared): [5.26, 10.1]. Point estimate: 6.91 mg/mL.

common_failure_modes: Using z-based CI for the mean instead of χ²-based CI for the variance
Reporting the sample SD (6.91) as the population SD without uncertainty
Not recognizing that confidence intervals for variance are asymmetric
